definition: A standalone, local-first memory layer for AI coding agents and related tooling.
A durable stored memory unit managed by Mnemix.
A high-priority memory item that should be favored during retrieval.
Deeper memory that is not loaded by default and is expanded on demand.
A retrieval strategy that loads the most relevant compact context first and expands only when needed.
A human-readable named reference to a stable storage version.
Read or inspect a prior state without making it the current head.
Create a new current state from a selected historical state.
The planned application-facing local storage layer for Mnemix.
The planned lower-level dataset layer used for advanced version-control-style workflows.
A boundary used to organize and retrieve memory in context-aware ways.
The retrieval flow that returns ranked memory relevant to a query or scope.
